mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 tyrosine kinase (targets FLT3‑ITD)
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3‑mutant AML; expected IC50 in low nM for FLT3‑ITD lines (MOLM‑13). Recommended 10‑point 1:3 dilution series.

mechanism: Type II FLT3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; potent against FLT3‑ITD; expected IC50 low‑to‑sub‑100 nM in sensitive lines.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 including D835 mutants
additional_notes: Activity reported against FLT3‑ITD/D835; active in MOLM lines.

mechanism: Multikinase inhibitor including FLT3 and PKC
additional_notes: Clinically used in FLT3‑mutated AML; activity in MOLM‑13.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases (including off‑target FLT3)
additional_notes: Higher top concentration recommended due to lower potency in some AML cells; multikinase activity.

mechanism: Tyrosine kinase inhibitor active against BCR‑ABL and FLT3 variants
additional_notes: Potent TKI with reported activity against FLT3 variants.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 inhibitor inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; expected IC50 often in the tens of nM in sensitive lines; can be synergistic with other agents.

mechanism: Reversible proteasome (26S) inhibitor
additional_notes: Proteasome inhibition can kill AML cells; some MOLM‑13 sensitivity reported.

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome inhibitor (chymotrypsin‑like activity)
additional_notes: Irreversible inhibitor with leukemia model activity.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK1/2/5/9, suppressing transcriptional CDKs (CDK9) and lowering short‑lived survival proteins such as MCL‑1
additional_notes: Expected IC50 often in low‑to‑tens of nM in AML lines.

mechanism: Transcriptional CDK inhibitor (targets CDK9 and others), reduces MCL‑1
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol; clinical history in AML.

mechanism: Pan‑histone deacetylase inhibitor, modulates chromatin and gene expression
additional_notes: Epigenetic agent; may require longer exposures for some effects.

mechanism: Histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDAC)
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; clinically used HDAC inhibitor with activity in some AML contexts.

mechanism: Inhibits BET bromodomains (BRD proteins), downregulates MYC and other transcriptional drivers
additional_notes: Also known as MK‑8628; preclinical activity in AML models.

mechanism: HSP90 inhibitor that destabilizes client oncoproteins (e.g., FLT3)
additional_notes: Also called tanespimycin; reported preclinical AML activity.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, inducing oxidative/pro‑oxidant stress
additional_notes: Gold compound with reported activity in AML including MOLM‑13; repurposing candidate.

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial function and modulates multiple signaling pathways
additional_notes: Reported in vitro anti‑AML activity; multi‑pathway effects, repurposing candidate.

mechanism: Microtubule destabilizer (binds tubulin)
additional_notes: Has shown anti‑leukemia activity in preclinical models.

mechanism: Forms complexes with copper that inhibit proteasome/other targets and aldehyde dehydrogenases, causing cell death
additional_notes: Requires copper co‑treatment (e.g., 1–10 µM CuCl2) for activity in many assays; test ±Cu to confirm dependence.

mechanism: Lysosomal/autophagy inhibitor (raises lysosomal pH, blocks autophagic flux)
additional_notes: Single‑agent effects modest; can sensitize AML cells to other agents; higher top conc recommended (20 µM).
